page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 4 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance primarily in the weak to average tiers. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, rank in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is weak, though large block read and write operations achieve average tier results. Cryptographic operations and extended instruction sets perform at an average level, as demonstrated by Passmark benchmarks. Redis SET operations also maintain average throughput, while static web serving and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the lower performance percentiles.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio offer predictable compute performance for workloads that do not require high-frequency processors or local NVMe storage. Given its performance profile, the instance is appropriate for general-purpose workloads, lightweight application hosting, Redis caching, and basic database operations that do not demand high IOPS or extreme single-threaded speed.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8163 x86_64 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate weak to average performance, with single-core and multi-core CPU tests scoring in the bottom 25%, while large-block memory bandwidth and Passmark encryption tests reach average levels. This instance offers predictable dedicated CPU resources for workloads that do not require high-frequency processing or local NVMe storage. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, Redis caching, and moderate database workloads.
